title: calyptocarpus tampicanus
intro: Calyptocarpus tampicanus, also known as Mexican mint or False Pennyroyal, is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like linalool and geranyl acetate, showing antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, Calyptocarpus tampicanus is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
